danger_signals.visual_form: Heavily armed thugs with distinct visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Organized antagonist faction; armed threat requiring combat engagement
Biological/supernatural threat; offspring of primary antagonist Carnage

danger_signals.visual_form: Repeated sprite variations with 'washed out faces' (per reviews)
danger_signals.meaning: Common street-level danger; visual simplicity communicates lower-tier threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Confined spaces with multiple spawn points
danger_signals.meaning: Threat density; player surrounded by simultaneous enemies indicating danger escalation

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban environments; city-based locations matching Spider-Man's domain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Symbiote organic texture; fanged mouth design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground locations; Life Foundation headquarters; symbiote-infested areas
Life Foundation headquarters; underground city; contaminated/organic-overgrown areas
Life Foundation facilities; organized institutional locations
Headquarters structure; underground research facilities; San Francisco locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: San Francisco urban environments; digger machine location (per Lethal Protector source material)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Thugs present but manageable; investigation-focused encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underground city becomes visible/accessible; shift from surface to subterranean environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density increases; symbiote-based enemies appear; threat escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five symbiote seed creatures become visible; environmental corruption increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple symbiote variants appear as playable challenges; biological threat manifestation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life Foundation headquarters becomes primary visual focus; corporate aesthetic dominates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jury and corporate thugs increase; organized military-style opposition

world_state_to_visual.world_state: End game - Escape and final confrontation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life Foundation environment under siege; visual instability from symbiote presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemy types present; maximum threat density; Carnage final boss encounter imminent

environmental_storytelling.example: San Francisco surface → underground city → Life Foundation headquarters progression mirrors story escalation from investigation to confrontation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type distribution as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Early thugs-only encounters → symbiote children appearances → Jury presence indicates shift from street-level to institutional threat
Underground city and Life Foundation environments show organic overgrowth indicating Carnage/symbiote influence on physical world

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character roster cameos as alliance indicators
environmental_storytelling.example: Captain America, Ghost Rider, Hawkeye, Daredevil appearances signal broader Marvel universe awareness of conflict; temporary alliance manifestation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Agile movement patterns; web-based offense (implied by character kit); reactive positioning
Aggressive close-range offense; symbiote-based physical attacks; offensive positioning preference
Organized formation attacks; armed assault patterns; coordinated multiple-enemy tactics
Repetitive attack patterns; spawn wave behavior; constant flow of unavoidable hits (per reviews)
Biological/organic attack patterns; symbiote-specific abilities; biological threat manifestation
End-game encounter; maximum threat level; symbiote-enhanced combat abilities

notes: This beat-em-up communicates threat through enemy visual repetition and variety rather than detailed sprite work. Reviews noted graphics are 'dated' and 'lack detail,' but faction identity remains clear through character design (red/blue for Spider-Man, black/white for Venom, red/black for Carnage). World-state progression follows Lethal Protector comic arc: investigation → underground discovery → institutional confrontation. Environmental storytelling relies on location shifts (surface → underground → HQ) rather than dynamic visual changes. Safety signals manifest through Marvel cameo allies providing temporary assistance. The game's visual communication strategy prioritizes character and enemy-type differentiation over environmental detail, typical of 1995 SNES-era beat-em-ups.
sources_used: tcrf - Separation Anxiety (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
